Title: Not explicitly provided in supplied text; review on molecular mechanisms and breeding strategies of rice cooking and eating quality
Journal: Molecular Plant Pathology? No; journal not explicitly shown in supplied text

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhennuo background transgenic lines; Wuyunjing 8; Zhenshan 97; Minghui 63; Nipponbare; 9311; Nanyangzhan; Nangeng series; XY99; JXY1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary target for CEQ improvement. Select or edit Wx alleles according to regional texture preference; use marker-assisted introgression or CRISPR editing of promoter/intron/exon/uORF elements to fine-tune amylose and pleiotropic starch-protein-lipid balance. Gene pyramiding with ALK and GS3 is recommended. Caution: overly strong or weak Wx function can impair preferred texture, transparency, or other quality traits depending on market class.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines amylose content and broadly pleiotropically regulates endosperm starch, protein, lipid composition, grain transparency, viscosity traits, and taste value, making it the central determinant of rice cooking and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural allele comparison; transgenic lines carrying distinct Wx alleles in a common Zhennuo background; QTL mapping; GWAS; physicochemical trait profiling; GBSSI activity assays from cited studies
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen fertilization level; temperature during grain development; standard field growth conditions in 2023 season at Huazhong Agricultural University farm
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: RNA methylation in starch metabolism is proposed as a future direction, but no direct epigenetic mechanism for Wx is established in this review
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in rice endosperm during grain filling; mature 2.3 kb mRNA level associated with storage substance traits
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NF-YB1; NF-YC12; bHLH144; RISBZ2; OsMADS1nyz (regulatory connection); OsSPL14/IPA1; OsNF-YB9; OsNF-YC8-12; OsCCA1; OsLHY; Nhd1
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS; genome-wide association analyses of protein and lipid traits; no accession numbers reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes at least nine natural alleles including wx, Wxa, Wxin, Wxb, Wxla/Wxmw, Wxmp, Wxmq, Wxop/Wxhp, Wxiv and mentions novel Wxela. Functional variation arises via splicing efficiency, amino acid substitutions, starch granule binding, enzyme activity changes, and intragenic recombination. Same Wx allele can still show CEQ variation due to amylopectin, glutelin, and lipid networks.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low-amylose types often show opaque/dull grains, though Wxla/Wxmw can maintain transparency; stronger Wx function worsens eating quality under some conditions; edits increasing resistant starch can reduce CEQ when combined with amylopectin-pathway perturbation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Granule-bound starch synthase I with GT1 glycosyltransferase domain
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Multiple cultivated and wild rice accessions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx is the central hub gene for rice CEQ by controlling amylose synthesis through GBSSI. 2. Natural allelic variation alters splicing, GBSSI activity, starch-granule binding, and amylose content, producing major texture differences. 3. Wx also pleiotropically affects amylopectin, storage proteins, lipids, grain transparency, viscosity traits, and taste value. 4. Fine-tuning Wx through breeding or genome editing is the most direct route to improve CEQ.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of cooked rice hardness, fluffiness, stickiness, viscosity, and regional consumer preference

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: General rice germplasm discussed in review
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful indirect target for aroma improvement; breeding should combine fragrance with CEQ and yield. Mutant alleles can raise 2AP but must be balanced with agronomic performance.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or reduction of BADH2 activity allows accumulation of 2AP precursor and promotes fragrance, an important component of CEQ.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural allele analyses and evolutionary comparisons summarized from cited studies
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cooking process contributes to 2AP formation from accumulated precursor
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Evolutionary analysis of allelic variants; no accession numbers reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes 19 major BADH2 allelic variants such as badh2-E2 and badh2-E7; active BADH2 converts GABald to GABA in non-fragrant rice, while mutant BADH2 permits 2AP formation. Evolutionary analysis suggests fragrance genes first appeared in japonica.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not detailed here; fragrance breeding must consider yield and other quality traits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BADH2 is the core fragrance gene in rice. 2. Functional BADH2 removes the 2AP precursor, whereas mutant badh2 alleles allow 2AP accumulation and aroma formation. 3. BADH2 allelic diversity underlies fragrant rice types and contributes to CEQ beyond starch traits.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers fragrant aroma and improves sensory eating quality

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in natural alleles; CRISPR target sites in promoter/intron/exon/uORF mentioned; specific promoters not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhennuo background transgenic lines and multiple cultivars/accessions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Premier engineering target for tailoring amylose level and CEQ. Deploy allele introgression (e.g., Wxmp, Wxmw) or genome editing of cis-elements/coding sites to create market-specific grain quality. Best used with ALK and other starch/protein regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate essentially linear alpha-1,4-glucan chains forming amylose within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amylose accumulation; allelic weakening lowers amylose and often improves softness and palatability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allelic analysis; GBSSI activity comparisons; in vitro activity/binding evidence from cited studies; transgenic allele series; metabolite/quality profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen level; temperature during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct evidence in this review
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NF-YB1; NF-YC12; bHLH144; RISBZ2; OsGBSSI chaperoned by OsHsp70-2; OsHsp40-1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS and trait association analyses; no accession numbers reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Different alleles alter function via splice-site SNPs, exon SNPs, reduced starch-granule binding, or GT1-domain changes; novel Wxela lowers GBSSI activity. Strong pleiotropy extends beyond starch to protein and lipids.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose branch of endosperm starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak alleles may cause opaque grain in some backgrounds; over- or under-expression can move texture away from target consumer preference
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major key control step for amylose content
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Multiple allelic backgrounds including Wxa, Wxb, Wxin, Wxmw, Wxmp, Wxela
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx encodes GBSSI, the principal enzyme for amylose synthesis. 2. Natural and induced alleles generate broad CEQ variation by changing expression, splicing, catalytic activity, or starch-granule binding. 3. Fine-tuning Wx is central to CEQ breeding and editing.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shapes rice texture, viscosity, hardness, and consumer preference

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: General rice germplasm; 9311 donor allele introgressed into Nipponbare mentioned
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important companion target with Wx for CEQ optimization. Introgress favorable ALK allele or edit to lower GT and improve softness while matching target amylose background.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates amylopectin intermediate chains, especially medium chains of DP13-25
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls gelatinization temperature and amylopectin fine structure; weaker alleles increase short chains and reduce GT, usually improving cooking quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; allele comparison; introgression studies; starch chain-length profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes target product structure rather than directly forming final CEQ phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream control step in amylopectin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: At least four alleles affect GT: ALKc and ALKd confer high GT, ALKa and ALKb confer low GT.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mismatch with desired market texture if GT is too high or too low
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for gelatinization temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALK/SSIIa is a major determinant of gelatinization temperature. 2. It elongates amylopectin medium chains, thereby altering starch thermal properties. 3. Favorable alleles improve CEQ and are widely useful in breeding with Wx.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amylopectin fine structure affecting starch gelatinization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines softness, gel consistency, and cooking behavior

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential improvement target to shift chain-length distribution toward better CEQ by moderate downregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates amylopectin branches; specific contribution mainly to short-chain distribution in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Japonica SSI alleles produce shorter amylopectin chains and better ECQ than indica SSI; absence/downregulation alters chain-length distribution without major structural defects
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes structural features of starch influencing CEQ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss does not markedly affect starch granule morphology because of compensation by other SSs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional compensation may limit effect size; excessive suppression could perturb starch properties unpredictably
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSI modulates amylopectin chain-length distribution. 2. Japonica SSI alleles are associated with better eating quality than indica alleles. 3. Functional redundancy limits major developmental defects upon loss.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppressing OsSSIIb is proposed to improve cooking quality by shifting amylopectin chain distribution and reducing Wx/OsSSIIa expression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in elongation of amylopectin chains contributing to medium-chain distribution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation reduces medium chains (DP13-24), increases short chains (DP5-12), and improves rice cooking quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene downregulation with starch chain-length and expression analyses
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes structural determinants of starch quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its downregulation also lowers Wx and OsSSIIa expression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May influence multiple starch genes and broader starch properties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSSIIb affects amylopectin chain balance. 2. Its downregulation increases short chains and improves cooking quality. 3. It also influences Wx and OsSSIIa expression, indicating network effects.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for resistant starch and quality modulation, but edits may increase resistant starch at the expense of CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes long amylopectin B-chains, especially DP >35
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of OsSSIIIa increases amylose, resistant starch, lipids, amylose-lipid complexes, and gelatinization temperature
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies structural branches that alter starch architecture and downstream quality
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functional overlap with SSIVb; redundancy with OsSSIIIb/SS3b context discussed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SSIIIa and SSIVb overlap in synthesizing branches DP≥42; alteration may improve resistant starch but hurt CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher resistant starch and GT may reduce eating quality
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSSIIIa extends long amylopectin chains. 2. Mutation elevates amylose, resistant starch, lipids, and GT. 3. It is a useful but tradeoff-prone target for nutritional starch modification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences thermal behavior, digestibility, and CEQ

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Minor target for starch architecture engineering; likely less impactful alone for CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to initiation/transient starch granules and elongation of very long amylopectin chains in SSIVb form
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SSIVb elongates long amylopectin chains; SSIVa/b overall do not strongly affect endosperm starch content/structure alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional overlap with SSIIIa in synthesis of branches DP≥42.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIVb contributes to long-chain amylopectin elongation. 2. Its role overlaps with SSIIIa. 3. Alone it has limited effect on overall endosperm starch properties.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter branch density and amylopectin short-chain composition for CEQ engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves alpha-1,4 glucan chains and transfers chain segments to C6 hydroxyls to form alpha-1,6 branches in amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SBEIIa generates DP6-12 short chains; SBEIIb, an endosperm-specific isoform, controls DP6-7 short chains, strongly affecting amylopectin fine structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical and mutant evidence summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Builds branch architecture of target starch polymer
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SBEII enzymes are much more efficient than SBEI for forming short chains.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong perturbation may impair normal starch packing and CEQ balance
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Branched amylopectin chains with alpha-1,6 linkages
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEIIb is an endosperm-specific branching enzyme shaping very short amylopectin chains. 2. It is central to amylopectin fine structure. 3. Manipulating branching enzymes can modify CEQ, though specific breeding outcomes require balance.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for correcting amylopectin structure; likely most useful in combination with other starch genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes improper alpha-1,6 branches in glucans to enable crystalline amylopectin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for proper amylopectin biosynthesis; ISA1 hetero-oligomer predominates in rice endosperm, with homooligomer as supplement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical oligomer analysis and pathway studies summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper assembly of target starch polymer
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ISA1 forms homo- and hetero-oligomers; hetero-oligomer is dominant in endosperm amylopectin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption can severely perturb starch structure
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required step but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ISA1 edits amylopectin branching to ensure proper starch assembly. 2. Hetero-oligomeric ISA complexes dominate in rice endosperm. 3. Debranching enzymes complement branching and synthase activities in CEQ-related starch architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal crystallinity and cooking properties

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary target because of compensatory relationship with ISA.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,6 branch linkages in glucans/pullulan-like substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Compensates for ISA activity in debranching during starch metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional pathway analysis summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts mainly on alpha-1,6-linked branches and has compensatory effect on ISA.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely modest alone due to redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PUL is a debranching enzyme with compensatory function relative to ISA. 2. It removes alpha-1,6 branches during starch metabolism. 3. It contributes indirectly to starch quality architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects starch structure and cooking properties indirectly

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockdown or knockout can elevate oleic acid and alter rice lipid quality; useful for oil-quality and CEQ-related lipid engineering, but should be balanced against broader lipid-function changes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond into oleic acid to produce linoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression increases oleic acid and decreases linoleic and palmitic acid levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression and fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with oleic-acid accumulation branch and controls unsaturated fatty acid composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genome editing knockout was noted as generating high-unsaturated-fatty-acid rice in the review text, though the classic biochemical expectation from FAD2 loss is increased oleic acid with reduced linoleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter membrane or storage lipid properties and texture/aroma balance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in oleic/linoleic partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFAD2-1 controls the oleic-to-linoleic acid conversion branch. 2. Suppression raises oleic acid and lowers linoleic acid. 3. It is an important engineering target for rice lipid composition and potentially CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences pasting behavior, texture, and lipid nutritional quality

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for increasing omega-3 ALA and modulating unsaturated fatty acid profile related to rice quality and nutrition.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts linoleic acid to alpha-linolenic acid by further desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes production of ALA and contributes to global unsaturated fatty acid composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional assignment summarized from cited literature
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target unsaturated fatty acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states SLL1 regulates global fatty acid desaturation and OsFAD3 catalyzes linoleic acid to ALA conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in fatty acid unsaturation can affect grain stability and flavor chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFAD3 converts linoleic acid to ALA. 2. It contributes to unsaturated fatty acid quality in rice. 3. It is relevant to nutritional and CEQ-associated lipid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to nutritional quality and volatile flavor precursor pool

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target linking lipid-derived surface metabolites and yield; may be useful where grain lipid-associated quality and productivity are jointly optimized.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Affects cutin monomer synthesis; exact enzymatic reaction not detailed in this review
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield; identified by mGWAS and pGWAS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies or modifies lipid-derived metabolite pool relevant to grain traits
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: mGWAS; pGWAS; no accession numbers reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a gene regulated by OsMADS6/OsMADS17 affecting cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter yield-related epidermal or grain-surface traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FGC3 was identified by metabolite- and phenotype-GWAS. 2. It influences cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield. 3. It is regulated by OsMADS6/OsMADS17 and represents a lipid-related CEQ candidate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May affect grain properties and quality indirectly

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying VLCFA composition and linked cytokinin metabolism, but likely pleiotropic on growth.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes elongation steps in very-long-chain fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates very-long-chain fatty acids and influences cytokinin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for VLCFA-derived lipids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Links fatty acid elongation with cytokinin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hormonal pleiotropy may affect plant architecture or yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKCS11 regulates VLCFA biosynthesis. 2. It also influences cytokinin metabolism, indicating broader developmental roles. 3. It is a pleiotropic lipid-metabolism candidate in rice quality biology.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lipid composition and associated structural traits

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for maintaining grain oil bodies and triglyceride accumulation; manipulation may affect seed vigor and lipid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Structural oil-body protein rather than catalytic enzyme; helps package/maintain triacylglycerol bodies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of OLE16/OLE18 causes oil body swelling and decreases triglyceride levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene suppression and lipid phenotype observation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target lipid product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structural protein controlling oil-body morphology rather than catalytic conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced triglycerides and potential seed-quality defects upon suppression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Storage determinant, not catalytic rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OLE16 helps maintain normal oil-body structure. 2. Its suppression causes oil-body swelling and lower TAG. 3. It is important for rice seed lipid storage quality.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage and contributor to grain lipid traits

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to reduce protein content and improve taste while coordinating starch traits. Could be combined with CEQ genes in breeding for balanced quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates rice protein content and taste value by modulating storage-protein and amino acid transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-relevant regulator of grain protein accumulation affecting CEQ
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein biosynthesis and coordinated endosperm storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of OsGATA7-SMOS1 module; also changes endosperm starch.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Modulates expression of protein biosynthesis and related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGATA7 with SMOS1 regulates grain protein content and taste value. 2. It controls OsGluA2, OsAAP6, RM1, and OsGluD. 3. The module also influences starch, coordinating storage substance accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects cooked rice hardness, gelatinization interference, and eating quality

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding target for lowering grain protein and improving eating quality while supporting yield-related traits depending on context; useful in quality-yield balancing modules.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates grain protein content while positively regulating amylose content and eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and interaction studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Antagonistic major switch balancing starch and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNAC42; OsNF-Y complex (interaction context in review)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Balance between starch accumulation and storage protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms heterodimer with OsNAC42 to antagonistically regulate starch/protein genes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a pleiotropic agronomic regulator, may affect heading date/yield-related traits, though not detailed here
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Starch genes; protein genes (specific direct targets not fully listed)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimer with OsNAC42; interacts functionally with OsNF-Y-centered network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes starch/amylose-related program and represses protein accumulation program
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ghd7 reduces grain protein content and improves eating quality. 2. It antagonizes OsNAC42 through heterodimer formation. 3. Ghd7 is a key module balancing starch and protein for quality and yield.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to tune starch-protein balance opposite to Ghd7; likely useful only with careful balancing because excessive activity may worsen CEQ by increasing protein.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exerts effects opposite to Ghd7, increasing protein-associated traits and reducing eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic interaction module summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major antagonistic regulator of storage protein versus starch balance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein and starch accumulation balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognized as key regulatory module governing storage proteins and starch in seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can worsen eating quality if protein accumulation is excessive
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Opposes Ghd7 on starch/protein gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC42 acts antagonistically to Ghd7 in rice seed storage regulation. 2. It promotes the opposite starch/protein balance relative to Ghd7. 3. Together they form a heterodimeric module affecting CEQ and yield balance.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central network target for coordinated improvement of protein and starch accumulation, but pleiotropy requires precise tuning rather than full knockout.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SSP and starch genes, regulates ER protein processing, and controls chalk-related genes, thereby shaping CEQ
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important coordinator linking storage protein synthesis, starch synthesis, and ER processing
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein biosynthesis, starch biosynthesis, ER protein processing, chalkiness-related quality formation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a key factor interacting with OsNF-Y-centered storage-regulation network.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation may induce ER stress and chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SSP genes; starch genes; OsBIP1; PDIL1-1; chalk-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SSP and starch genes and ER folding machinery
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP60 coordinately activates seed storage protein and starch genes. 2. It links ER protein processing with endosperm storage accumulation. 3. It influences chalk-related quality traits and CEQ.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determine rice texture and grain appearance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ER stress/developmental signals not detailed here

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for coordinated tuning of seed protein and starch, though redundancy suggests stacked editing with OsNAC26 may be needed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SSP and starch genes; double mutation with OsNAC26 reduces both starch and SSPs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analyses summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant activator pair essential for full accumulation of starch and seed storage proteins
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive and negative loops with OsNAC25 via upstream regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutation has minor effect due to redundancy with OsNAC26.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double suppression reduces both starch and proteins strongly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SSP and starch biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC20 is a positive regulator of both SSP and starch genes. 2. It functions redundantly with OsNAC26. 3. Their combined action is important for endosperm storage accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Companion target with OsNAC20 for coordinated storage regulation; editing alone may have limited effect because of redundancy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with OsNAC20 to activate SSP and starch genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analyses summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential redundant activator in seed storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Minor single-mutant effect due to redundancy.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined mutation reduces both starch and SSPs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC26 works with OsNAC20 to activate seed storage programs. 2. Redundancy buffers single-gene perturbation. 3. The pair is important for starch and protein accumulation relevant to CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for fine-tuning starch accumulation through network balancing rather than strong perturbation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upstream regulator of OsNAC20/26 that forms positive and negative loops with them to balance starch gene expression and accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory network analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Balancer of starch gene expression within NAC module
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive and negative feedback loops with OsNAC20/26
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Balancing starch gene expression and accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specifically positioned upstream of OsNAC20/26 in review's storage regulatory network.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Network perturbation may destabilize storage balance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsNAC20; OsNAC26; downstream starch genes indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Balances activation outputs of OsNAC20/26 and starch genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC25 lies upstream of OsNAC20/26. 2. It forms both positive and negative loops with them. 3. This module balances starch gene expression and storage accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhenshan 97 high-expression allele introgressed into Nanyangzhan mentioned
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable breeding target for nutritional enhancement and coordinated starch-protein tuning; introgression of high-expression allele increases protein but may alter CEQ, so deployment depends on breeding goal.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts seed storage protein synthesis by transporting amino acids and shifts starch metabolism toward higher amylose and lower amylopectin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL/gene cloning and introgression studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key transporter-based determinant of grain protein content and starch-protein partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amino acid transport, seed storage protein accumulation, and starch gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promotes GBSS/SS/DBE and inhibits BE according to review.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher protein can negatively affect CEQ even while improving nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transport control with indirect transcriptional/metabolic effects
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes GBSS/SS/DBE and inhibits BE, increasing amylose and reducing amylopectin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPC1 encodes OsAAP6, an amino acid transporter increasing grain protein accumulation. 2. It also shifts starch metabolism toward more amylose and less amylopectin. 3. It is useful for nutritional improvement but must be balanced against CEQ effects.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for understanding chalkiness-quality link, but generally a negative target because high expression increases chalkiness.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chalk formation, abnormal amylose accumulation, and lower protein, thereby influencing starch-protein balance and CEQ
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong inducer of chalkiness and storage-substance imbalance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat response context implied in chalkiness-related quality section
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chalkiness formation and storage metabolism gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes it upregulates 12 storage metabolism genes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increases chalkiness and can worsen appearance quality despite altered protein/starch balance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Twelve storage metabolism genes (not individually listed)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vacuolar H+-translocating pyrophosphatase family (known identity, not explicitly stated in review)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of storage metabolism/chalkiness-associated pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates multiple storage metabolism genes; increases amylose; lowers protein
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chalk5 promotes chalkiness and perturbs endosperm storage composition. 2. It increases amylose and gel consistency but lowers protein. 3. It is a major negative factor for appearance and CEQ balance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects grain appearance and cooking texture

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value CEQ target: favorable alleles reduce chalkiness and protein while increasing starch and eating quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces white-core rate and protein (mainly glutelin), while increasing starch, amylose, and eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator improving chalkiness and storage-balance-associated CEQ
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chalkiness suppression and storage substance partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main protein reduction reported is glutelin, the storage fraction most negatively associated with taste value.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of starch accumulation / repression of chalkiness-associated protein deposition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WCR1 reduces white-core chalkiness. 2. It lowers protein, especially glutelin, while increasing starch and amylose. 3. These changes improve rice eating quality.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Nipponbare fragment introgression context; nyz allele in qGT3 study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful pleiotropic target for grain shape, sugar transport, starch/protein balance, and CEQ. Fine-tuning via natural alleles or editing is preferable to knockout.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates grain thickness, sugar transport, starch-gene expression, and CEQ; certain alleles suppress Wx to reduce amylose/protein and improve eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant studies; qTL cloning; regulatory assays summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major developmental regulator with strong pleiotropic control over grain filling and storage balance
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MST4; OsNF-Y complex; FGC3 regulators OsMADS6/17 are separate
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Grain shape, sugar transport during filling, starch/protein accumulation, CEQ formation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsMADS1 mutations disrupt starch-gene expression and impair eating quality; OsMADS1nyz activates MST4 and suppresses Wx.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function impairs eating quality and grain filling; pleiotropy on morphology
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MST4; suppresses Wx in qGT3 context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS1 is a pleiotropic regulator of grain shape, filling, and CEQ. 2. Through qGT3 it activates MST4 and can repress Wx, reducing amylose/protein while increasing starch and eating quality. 3. Loss of OsMADS1 disrupts starch gene expression and impairs quality.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines cooked rice texture and grain quality

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential network hub for balancing starch and protein; useful when coordinated with NF-Y partners.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss reduces starch and amylose but raises protein; regulates Wx and PDIL1-1 with OsNF-YB9/YC8-12 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function and complex studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important complex-based regulator of endosperm starch and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNF-YB9; OsNF-YC8; OsNF-YC9; OsNF-YC10; OsNF-YC11; OsNF-YC12
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of starch and protein accumulation in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also listed among NUE-related genes validated to influence grain protein content.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropy on plant architecture/yield likely, though not detailed here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in a complex with OsNF-YB9 and OsNF-YC8-12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSPL14/IPA1 influences starch and protein accumulation in rice endosperm. 2. It works with OsNF-YB9/YC8-12 to regulate Wx and PDIL1-1. 3. Loss of function lowers starch/amylose and raises protein, harming CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core network component for engineering coordinated starch/protein accumulation and temperature-responsive quality stability.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with NF-YB1, binds FLO6 and OsGS1;3 promoters, and with bHLH144 stabilizes NF-YB1 to activate Wx
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and promoter binding studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal OsNF-Y complex component in rice seed storage regulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Temperature response implicated through NF-Y network
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activates Wx indirectly through NF-YB1 stabilization; also regulates OsSUT1 in NF-YB1 partnership per review.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complex pleiotropy; likely sensitive to environmental conditions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: NF-Y complex with NF-YB1 and bHLH144-supported stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and protein stabilization within complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsSUT1-related module and Wx; regulates FLO6 and OsGS1;3 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YC12 is a pivotal OsNF-Y complex member for seed storage regulation. 2. It partners with NF-YB1 and bHLH144 to activate Wx and other storage genes. 3. It links sugar transport and starch/protein accumulation in rice endosperm.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important NF-Y hub for improving CEQ through coordinated sugar transport and Wx activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with NF-YC12; regulated/stabilized by bHLH144; activates OsSUT1 and contributes to Wx activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and promoter studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central NF-Y hub connecting sugar transport and starch synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Temperature response implied via NF-Y network
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of pivotal OsNF-Y regulatory complex emphasized by review.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: NF-Y complex with NF-YC12 and bHLH144 support
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsSUT1 and supports Wx activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YB1 is a central component of the OsNF-Y storage-regulation complex. 2. Together with NF-YC12 and bHLH144 it regulates OsSUT1 and activates Wx. 3. It integrates filling and starch-quality control.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter and edited promoter mentioned for OsNF-YC4
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter editing can alter protein/starch balance; likely useful for fine-tuning rather than strong upregulation because increased expression raises protein and reduces starch.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression raises protein and lowers starch
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes promoter editing raises protein and lowers starch.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNF-YC4 expression level affects endosperm protein and starch balance. 2. Increased expression raises protein but lowers starch. 3. Promoter editing offers a route to tune CEQ-related storage composition.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase aroma by repressing BADH2; promising if agronomic benefits noted are reproducible.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates BADH2, increasing 2AP and improving agronomic traits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator of fragrance through repression of fragrance-suppressing BADH2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aroma manifests during grain development/cooking context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states it boosts 2AP and agronomic traits by negatively regulating BADH2.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY19 promotes rice fragrance. 2. It acts by negatively regulating BADH2. 3. This raises 2AP while also benefiting agronomic traits according to the cited study.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances sensory quality of cooked rice

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for aroma improvement because knockout increases fatty-acid-derived volatiles without yield loss.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates fatty acid-derived volatiles via lipoxygenase pathway; knockout increases FAVs without yield loss
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-independent negative controller of FAV aroma branch with low apparent tradeoff
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Particularly attractive because review states no yield loss in knockout lines.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC021 represses fatty-acid-derived volatile production. 2. Knockout increases aroma without yield loss. 3. It is a promising CEQ improvement target outside the core starch pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves rice aroma and sensory quality
